title: Calotheca rotundata
taxonomy: Calotheca rotundata is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. This family consists of about 23 genera and 100 species, primarily found in the Americas. The plant is often classified as a member of the genus Calotheca, which is native to South America. Calotheca rotundata is further classified as a perennial herb, characterized by its rounded leaves and showy flowers. Its classification is closely related to that of other flowering plants in the same family, such as Acmadenia and Polemonium.

The plant's extracts have been studied for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which could be beneficial in addressing several diseases.
